commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads CLI session/yield context through bundled MCP loopback HTTP tool resolution and maps yielded CLI results to paused/end_turn metadata with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +66, Tests +82. Total +148 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main shows the MCP resolver omits the required `sessionId`/`onYield`, and the PR defect is source-reproducible by two same-session loopback yield runs within the 30-second cache TTL.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update the loopback cache lifecycle and add the missing consecutive-run regression coverage.

target.title: fix #77426: [Bug]:sessions_yield: always returns "No session context" on MCP/claude-cli agent runtime path (gateway tool resolver missing sessionId + onYield)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High confidence; the fix is bounded to the CLI bundle MCP loopback yield path, has RED/GREEN regression coverage, and has a local HTTP loopback smoke proof.
- Root cause: The root cause was a resolver/lifecycle context gap: the CLI bundle MCP config only carried routing context, MCP HTTP request context did not parse a run-scoped session id, and gateway scoped tool resolution dropped `sessionId`/`onYield` before `createOpenClawTools`, so `sessions_yield` was constructed without its required session lifecycle state.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch threads the same run `sessionId` from bundled MCP env/header through MCP HTTP request parsing, loopback tool cache identity, gateway scoped tool resolution, `createOpenClawTools`, and CLI run result lifecycle. `sessionKey` remains routing context, while `sessionId` is the source-of-truth key for the active run yield context.
- Patch quality notes: Patch quality warnings are addressed in scope. The default returns in `src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts` are fail-closed guard exits when no run session is registered; they do not mask the supported registered-session path. Related open PR / same-contract competition scan was handled by daily-fix issue selection before this work, and no competing canonical PR was adopted for issue #77426. The diff spans 7 source files and 4 test files because the session context crosses env/header, request parsing, resolver, cache identity, tool creation, and CLI result boundaries; each boundary has targeted regression coverage.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The run `sessionId` is the source-of-truth boundary for MCP loopback yield ownership; `sessionKey` stays a routing key. This changes an internal bundled MCP env/header contract only, not public user config, schema, migration, provider routing, or tool visibility defaults.

What problem does this PR solve?

`sessions_yield` on the bundled MCP / CLI loopback path did not receive run-scoped session context, so the tool could return `No session context` instead of yielding the active CLI-backed run.

Why does this matter now?

Issue #77426 reports that MCP/claude-cli agent runtime paths cannot use `sessions_yield` correctly, which breaks the pause/resume path expected after subagent work.

What is the intended outcome?

A loopback MCP `tools/call` for `sessions_yield` with the run session header returns `{ status: "yielded" }`, records yield state for the active CLI run, and exposes paused/end-turn result meta to downstream wait/lifecycle code.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change embedded-runner yield behavior, public user config, migrations, or tool visibility policy.

What does success look like?

CLI bundle MCP config carries `x-openclaw-session-id`; MCP request context and gateway scoped tool resolution preserve that session id and yield callback; CLI results are marked `yielded: true`, `livenessState: "paused"`, and `stopReason: "end_turn"` after loopback yield.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the process-local yield context ownership in `src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts`, the `sessionId` cache key in `src/gateway/mcp-http.runtime.ts`, and the CLI yielded/end-turn meta mapping in `src/agents/cli-runner.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #77426

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #77426

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Picked from the public bug issue queue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_yield` on the MCP/CLI loopback path no longer returns `No session context`; it records yield state for the active run.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with the real MCP loopback HTTP server and gateway tool resolver.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval '<start MCP loopback server, POST tools/call sessions_yield with x-openclaw-session-id, assert yielded context>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): After-fix terminal capture / copied live console output from the local MCP loopback smoke run.

  ```text
  Validation `http-loopback-sessions-yield-smoke` passed.
  HTTP POST /mcp tools/call sessions_yield returned:
  {"httpStatus":200,"rpcIsError":false,"toolResult":{"status":"yielded","message":"http smoke yield"},"yielded":true,"message":"http smoke yield"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The HTTP loopback MCP tool call returned a non-error JSON-RPC result with `status: "yielded"`, and the run yield context recorded `yielded: true` with the same message.
- What was not tested: A full external Claude CLI binary issuing the MCP call itself. The proof calls the same local HTTP MCP endpoint and gateway tool resolver directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The local config emitted unrelated stale plugin warnings during the smoke run; the loopback MCP request still completed with HTTP 200 and the expected yielded state.
- Before evidence: Added RED tests first. Before the production change, targeted tests showed missing `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, missing `sessionId` in MCP request/scoped resolver context, and no `onYield` callback invocation from gateway-scoped `sessions_yield`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`
  - Result: 10 test files passed across gateway/agents shards; 204 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src`
  - Result: passed.
- `node --import tsx --eval '<HTTP loopback sessions_yield smoke>'`
  - Result: passed with `httpStatus: 200`, `toolResult.status: "yielded"`, `yielded: true`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`: CLI bundle MCP env/config now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` and passes `sessionId` into prompt-time loopback scoped tool resolution.
- `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`: MCP HTTP headers now propagate `x-openclaw-session-id`, and loopback tool cache entries are separated by session id.
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts`: gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` receives `sessionId` + `onYield` and returns `status: "yielded"`.
- `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`: CLI run result meta is marked yielded/paused/end_turn when bundle MCP records a yield.

What failed before this fix, if known?

RED tests failed because the CLI env lacked `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, gateway request context passed `sessionId: undefined`, and gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` never invoked `onYield`.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression tests were added for the CLI prepare path, HTTP loopback path, gateway resolver behavior, and CLI run result meta.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. CLI-backed MCP `sessions_yield` can now pause a run instead of reporting missing session context.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Internal bundled MCP config/env now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` / `x-openclaw-session-id`. No user config or migration behavior changes.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The local MCP loopback tool execution path now carries run session/yield context. The existing bearer auth and loopback-only HTTP checks remain unchanged.

What is the highest-risk area?

Process-local yield state and cached loopback tool instances could leak across runs if not scoped correctly.

How is that risk mitigated?

Yield handlers are resolved by run `sessionId`; loopback tool cache keys include `sessionId`; and the CLI runner clears the exact registered yield context in `finally`.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author-side implementation, tests, and proof are complete locally; GitHub CI is the next external signal after the PR is created.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No bot or reviewer comments have been posted on this new PR yet.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
